turns.content: What brings you in today?

Well, I just returned from a long-haul flight assignment four days ago, and I've had non-stop diarrhea ever since. I'm talking watery stools about 8 to 10 times a day. I also can't keep even small sips of water down without vomiting. I feel incredibly weak and lightheaded, and it's really starting to scare me.
When did this start?

It started five days ago, actually while I was still on my layover overseas. The cramps and loose stools began gradually, but by the time I boarded the return flight, I was already running to the bathroom every hour.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

I feel cramping in my lower abdomen, sort of around my belly button and down toward my left side. It's a dull, gnawing ache that comes in waves, but it gets sharper right before I have to rush to the bathroom. There's also a constant feeling of nausea and an empty, hollow sensation in my stomach.
Have you had any fever or chills?

Yes, I've had a low-grade fever on and off, usually around 100 to 101 degrees. I've also had chills, especially at night — last night I was shivering under two blankets. Honestly, I'm more worried about the dehydration and weakness than the fever at this point.
What medications are you currently taking?

I take a low-dose beta blocker for mild hypertension, and I have an over-the-counter antacid I use occasionally for heartburn. I also started taking Imodium two days ago to try to slow things down, but it only helped for a few hours. Other than that, nothing else regularly.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?

I have mild hypertension, but it's well-controlled with my beta blocker. I also get occasional heartburn, but nothing serious. No diabetes, no thyroid issues, and no history of gastrointestinal problems like IBS or Crohn's. I've always been pretty healthy until this episode.
Are you allergic to any medications?

I'm allergic to penicillin — it gives me a rash, hives, and makes my throat feel tight. I also have a mild reaction to codeine; it makes me extremely nauseous and dizzy. Otherwise, no other known allergies.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

Well, I tried the Imodium like I mentioned, but that barely touched it. I've also been sipping water and electrolyte drinks, but I can't keep enough down to make a difference. I even tried some bland crackers and plain rice, but those came right back up within an hour. So honestly, I've stopped trying to eat much now.
Does anything make it better or worse?

Lying down flat seems to ease the cramping a little, but as soon as I sit up or move around, the nausea and urge to go come back. Eating or drinking anything definitely makes it worse — even a few sips of water will trigger another round of diarrhea or vomiting within minutes. The only thing that helps even slightly is just staying still and resting.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's completely derailed my life. I can't even stand long enough to shower without feeling like I might faint. I've had to call in sick for my next three shifts, which is a huge issue for my airline — we're short-staffed. I also haven't slept more than an hour at a time because I'm either running to the bathroom or shaking with chills. I'm honestly just exhausted and frustrated.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?

I don't smoke at all — never have. I do drink socially, maybe a glass of wine or a cocktail a couple times a week, but I haven't had a drop of alcohol since this started. The thought of it makes me nauseous.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?

No, no one in my immediate family has had anything like this recently. My parents are both in their 70s and have their own health issues, but nothing gastrointestinal. My two siblings are fine too. So I don't think there's any family pattern here — it's just me.
Have you experienced anything like this before?

No, never. I travel constantly for work, and I've had the occasional food poisoning or traveler's diarrhea that cleared up in a day or two. But nothing has ever gone on like this — five days, plus the vomiting and weakness — it's a whole different level. That's why I'm so worried.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?

Physically, I'd say it's really wearing me down — the cramps, the constant bathroom trips, the weakness. I feel like I've been run over. Emotionally, I'm honestly pretty anxious and irritable. I'm scared because I can't keep anything down and I don't know what's wrong, and I'm frustrated because it's keeping me from working and living normally. I'd say both are at a solid 7 out of 10 right now.
Is there anything else you think I should know?

Actually, I should mention that I was in Thailand and Vietnam on this last trip. I ate at some street stalls and had a few raw oysters at a local market. I remember thinking they might not have been the freshest, but I didn't think much of it then. I also drank tap water in one hotel by accident — just a few sips — but maybe that's related. I don't know if that helps, but I figured I'd mention it.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

Honestly, I've been trying to just rest when I can, but it's hard because every time I close my eyes, my stomach starts cramping again. I've also been drinking sports drinks whenever I can keep them down, but it's not really helping much. My husband has been checking on me, but I feel terrible making him worry and run around for me. I guess I'm just trying to push through, but I know I need help.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I've been trying to stay positive, but it's hard when I'm this weak and nothing I do helps. My husband brought me some ginger tea and crackers, but it just came right back up. Mostly I've been lying in bed, watching the clock, and feeling guilty for missing work. I'm honestly just hoping you can give me something to stop this, because I'm really at my limit.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I'm sorry, but you just asked me that twice now. I'm trying my best to answer your questions, but I'm feeling really worn down and lightheaded. As I said, I'm just lying in bed, barely keeping anything down, and hoping you can help me before I get worse. Could we focus on what I can do next?
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I'm sorry, but I'm getting a bit frustrated repeating myself. I'm not coping well at all — I'm weak, dehydrated, and scared. I came here because I need treatment, not to keep going over the same thing. Can we talk about what we can actually do to stop this?
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I understand you're trying to understand my situation, but I've told you several times now — I'm not coping well. I'm weak, dehydrated, and getting more concerned by the hour. What I really need is your medical advice on how to stop this diarrhea and vomiting so I can start recovering and get
